articles.description: AI data center developers are increasingly relying on a style of camp popularized as housing for men working in remote oil fields.

articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Rivian’s R2 gambit
articles.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for all things “future of transportation.”

articles.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
articles.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.

articles.title: Push for $40 smartphones builds momentum, but still faces cost hurdles
articles.description: A coalition of telecom operators and device makers is pushing $40 smartphones to bring up to 20 million people online, but rising component costs threaten the plan.

articles.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
articles.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

articles.title: Grammarly’s ‘expert review’ is just missing the actual experts
articles.description: A recently-added feature in Grammarly purports to improve users’ writing with help from the world's great writers and thinkers — and some tech journalists, too.

articles.title: OpenAI hardware exec Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
articles.description: Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's robotics team.

articles.title: PopSockets founder David Barnett talks about building a viral business
articles.description: David Barnett, founder of PopSocket, stops by Equity to talk about scaling the business and what he's learned as a leader.

articles.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
articles.description: The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.

articles.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
articles.description: The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.

articles.title: X is testing a new ad format that connects posts with products
articles.description: An ad test on X promotes Musk's Starlink beneath original content.

articles.title: Nintendo sues the US government for a refund on tariffs
articles.description: This lawsuit comes after a Supreme Court decision struck down some of the president's sweeping tariffs, which had impacted Nintendo and thousands of other companies.

articles.title: Life EV officially owns Rad Power Bikes now
articles.description: The Rad Power brand is expected to live on.

articles.title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
articles.description: A 61-year-old worker died on Thursday after reportedly getting stuck between a tractor trailer and a loading dock.

articles.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
articles.description: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

- Apple's 512GB Mac Studio quietly discontinued, signaling ongoing DRAM supply constraints that may reflect broader memory allocation priorities toward AI workloads across the industry
- PyPy maintenance status flagged as concerning by community - the alternative Python implementation's sustainability raises questions about JIT compilation ecosystem fragmentation
- Linux ported to PS5 as Steam Machine - demonstrates continued erosion of console walled gardens and homebrew/emulation pipeline maturation
- LibreOffice Writer adds Markdown support while simultaneously petitioning EU Commission on open document standards compliance - strategic positioning for public sector contracts
- SWE-CI benchmark release for evaluating AI agents on codebase maintenance via continuous integration - new evaluation framework as agent coding tools proliferate

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

- OpenAI hardware exec Caitlin Kalinowski resigns over Pentagon deal, second high-profile departure tied to defense contract controversy - internal fracture accelerating as safety-focused talent exits
- Sundar Pichai's $692M package includes new stock incentives explicitly tied to Waymo and Wing performance - Google signaling these are core bets, not science projects
- AI data center developers adopting remote "man camp" housing model from oil/gas sector - infrastructure buildout hitting labor logistics constraints in remote locations
- Robinhood's startup fund (Mercor, Ramp, Stripe exposure) stumbles on NYSE debut - retail appetite for private market access may be softer than anticipated
- Coalition pushing $40 smartphones to connect 20M users faces component cost headwinds - emerging market connectivity play hitting margin reality

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

- "Orchestration for zero-human companies" discussion on Lobste.rs alongside "Literate Programming in the Agent Era" - practitioners actively architecting for autonomous code generation and maintenance workflows
- Cloud VM benchmarks 2026 release provides updated cost/performance data across providers - critical input for infrastructure planning as compute allocation decisions tighten

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The OpenAI talent exodus over defense work, combined with Anthropic's Pentagon standoff, suggests the AI safety vs. deployment speed tension is now materially affecting org stability and talent retention - a key diligence factor for any AI-adjacent investment. Meanwhile, Apple's quiet SKU retirement and the man-camp infrastructure story both point to physical constraints (memory, labor, housing) becoming the real bottlenecks in the AI buildout cycle.
